column3: Graph schema constants: node/relationship tables, primary keys, allowed fields, CSV filenames and column orders, Cypher DDL for both node and relationship tables.
column4: Pure constants, no logic. Intentional duplication between `FIELD_MAP` (used for upsert validation) and `NODE_COLUMNS` (used for CSV column order) — they serve different purposes and should not be collapsed. The relationship DDL fixes description + git_revision as the only edge attributes; adding new attributes requires coordinated updates to DDL, CSV export in `_db.export_csv`, and MCP handler signatures.

column3: All KuzuDB (LadybugDB) operations: schema init, node and edge upsert/delete, stats, read-only query, CSV export, CSV rebuild.
column4: Each function accepts a `db_path` and opens a fresh `real_ladybug` Database + Connection — this is what makes the COW pattern work (the caller may pass a temp copy instead of the live path). `upsert_edge` must perform MERGE then SET in separate statements because KuzuDB cannot bind parameters in `SET` alongside `MERGE`. `delete_node` has two guardrail layers: Concepts with children are always refused (prevents orphans); any other incident edges require `detach=True`. `export_csv` emits headerless CSVs because `COPY FROM` expects headerless input — changing this breaks round-trips. `rebuild_from_csv` drops the existing `.db` and `.db.wal` before re-importing; node tables load before relationships.

column3: Copy-on-write lifecycle for the KuzuDB file: `begin_write` copies DB+WAL to a uuid-suffixed temp path; `commit_write` atomically renames back; `abort_write` cleans up on failure.
column4: Atomicity depends on `os.rename` being atomic on the same filesystem — if `.theo/db/` is ever a mountpoint boundary from its parent, this breaks. A stale WAL from the original is explicitly unlinked during commit when the tmp has no WAL, to keep the pair consistent. No parent-directory fsync; the CSVs are the durable source of truth and the `.db` is rebuildable, so a crash mid-rename is tolerable.

column3: Git helpers: `head_commit()` shells out to `git rev-parse HEAD`; `find_theo_root()` walks upward looking for `.theo/config.json`.
column4: `find_theo_root` caps traversal at 100 levels and tracks visited directories to survive circular symlinks — this guards the MCP server against hangs. `head_commit` returns `None` rather than raising when git is unavailable, so stats and freshness flows degrade gracefully outside a repo.

column3: Typer app registering `use`, `serve`, `stats`, `reload`, and `ui` subcommands. Installed as the `theo` console script via `pyproject.toml`.
column4: Each subcommand's implementation is lazy-imported inside the handler -- keeps `theo --help` fast and means optional deps (Flask, MCP) are only required when actually invoked. `no_args_is_help=True` on the Typer app.

column3: The MCP server. Loads `.theo/config.json`, ensures the DB is rebuilt (and embedding column migrated) when missing, then registers eight FastMCP tools over stdio: `theo_stats`, `theo_query`, `theo_search`, `theo_reload`, `theo_upsert_node`, `theo_upsert_edge`, `theo_delete_node`, `theo_delete_edge`.
column4:
Every write-path tool wraps a COW begin/commit/abort envelope and re-exports all CSVs on success so each successful write durably updates the versioned source of truth. Handler functions (`handle_theo_*`) are kept separate from the FastMCP registration so they can be unit-tested without spinning up the server.

`_ensure_db` both rebuilds from CSVs when the DB file is missing AND runs the idempotent embedding-column migration on every start — existing pre-semantic DBs gain the column without a full reload.

Auto-indexing: `_auto_index_node` / `_auto_index_edge` run AFTER the COW commit and CSV export. They are best-effort and wrap embedding in a broad `try/except` so the graph write and CSVs persist even if fastembed fails. A missing fastembed install is logged once at WARNING then silenced at DEBUG. The embedding model is pre-warmed in `run()` so the first write isn't blocked on cold-start load.

`handle_theo_search` validates + clamps `top_k` ([1, 1000]) before it reaches Cypher's `LIMIT`, since that value is f-string-interpolated. The generic except branch returns `str(exc)` to the MCP client: acceptable today because transport is local stdio only; if this ever becomes networked, redact there and log the traceback internally.

`theo_reload` lives inside MCP (not just as a CLI) so the rebuild runs in the same process that owns the DB handle — no external race with a live server when the coding assistant triggers a reindex from within its session.

`theo_stats` re-reads `config.json` on every call to keep `last_indexed_commit` fresh.

column3: `theo stats` — prints node/edge counts and freshness (last indexed commit vs. current HEAD) to the terminal.
column4: Read-only path; does not mutate the DB or CSVs. Shares freshness semantics with the MCP `theo_stats` tool: `is_stale` when `last_indexed_commit` is unset or differs from git HEAD.

column3: `theo use` -- idempotent project initialisation: `.theo/` layout, `.mcp.json`, `.codex/config.toml`, skill files under `.claude/` and `.agents/`, refreshed `AGENTS.md` section, `.gitignore` entry for `.theo/db/`.
column4: Text-based surgery for `.codex/config.toml` (regex header matching) and `AGENTS.md` (markdown heading matching) preserves unrelated user content on re-run. MCP registration embeds the absolute `project_dir` in the `theo serve` args so the server works regardless of the agent's cwd. Skill files are duplicated into `.claude/skills/theo/` and `.agents/skills/theo/` for cross-tool compatibility (Claude Code vs. Cursor/Codex). Preserves existing `config.json` on re-run to avoid clobbering `last_indexed_commit`. `AGENTS_MD_SECTION` is the single source of truth for the blurb injected into each project's AGENTS.md -- keep it aligned with the self-reflection protocol documented in SKILL.md.

column3: `theo ui` — Flask server rendering the graph with vis.js at 127.0.0.1:7777. Opens a browser tab automatically unless `--no-browser`.
column4: Large embedded HTML/CSS/JS template accounts for the file's size and the `E501` ruff exemption for this file. Every HTTP request opens a fresh read-only ladybug connection: the MCP server's COW atomic-rename means any cached DB handle would read stale data. Search endpoint is a stub returning empty results — semantic search will come later. Flask is an optional dep (`theo[ui]`); `_ensure_flask()` gives a helpful install hint when missing. `mypy` strict mode has `disallow_untyped_decorators = false` for this module because `flask.route` resolves to Any when Flask isn't installed in CI.

column3: Top-level package marker (`theo`). Intentionally empty — public API is the CLI, MCP tools, and UI; nothing is re-exported here.
CLI subpackage marker.
PEP 561 marker declaring the `theo` package ships inline type hints.
Skills subpackage; exists so `importlib.resources.files("theo.skills")` can locate the bundled markdown.
column4: Resource loading via `importlib.resources` only works because `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel].include` in `pyproject.toml` explicitly adds the non-Python markdown files to the wheel. Remove that line and skill files disappear at runtime.

column3: The canonical skill instruction copied into each target project. Documents the graph schema, MCP tools, session startup protocol, building/updating procedures, exploration and self-reflection protocols, and the `theo` trigger phrase.
column4: This is the prompt that actually drives coding-agent behavior -- changes here are effectively product changes to Theo. Must stay in sync with MCP tool signatures in `serve.py` (tool names, parameters, guardrails) or the agent will issue calls that don't match reality. The lens pipeline (architect -> interface designer -> dependency master -> criticality finder -> simplicity advocate) is the heart of the build/update loop. Two orthogonal protocols frame the agent's reading and writing behavior: Exploration (query the graph first, then grep) and Self-reflection (after any major exploration, register non-obvious discoveries in node/edge `notes`). The `AGENTS.md` blurb written by `theo use` mirrors this self-reflection cue.

column3: Shared pytest fixtures: `tmp_db` (just an initialised KuzuDB) and `tmp_theo_project` (full `.theo/` layout with DB, CSVs, and config).
column4: `tmp_theo_project` bypasses `theo use` and writes a minimal `config.json` directly — integration tests get a clean workspace without having to orchestrate the CLI.

column3: Unit tests for schema constants: table lists, PK map, field allowlists, DDL strings.
Unit tests for COW file lifecycle (begin/commit/abort, WAL handling).
Unit tests for `head_commit` and `find_theo_root`, including the circular-symlink guard.
Unit tests for the UI helpers (`_level_tier`, `_esc_html`, graph building, Flask routes).
Unit tests for the idempotent config-surgery helpers in `theo use` (TOML stripping, AGENTS.md section refresh, .mcp.json merging).
Integration tests against a real KuzuDB: schema init, upsert, delete, guardrails, CSV export/rebuild round-trip.
column4: Marked `integration` in `pyproject.toml` so CI can deselect with `-m 'not integration'` on cost-sensitive jobs.

column3: Integration tests that exercise the full COW envelope end-to-end against a live DB.
Integration tests for the MCP server handler functions (`handle_theo_*`) wiring COW, DB ops, and CSV export together.
End-to-end tests that shell out to the installed `theo` binary via subprocess.
column4: Marked `e2e` — slowest tier, depends on the CLI being on PATH.

column3: Tests package marker.
Unit-tests package marker.
Integration-tests package marker.
E2E-tests package marker.
Project metadata, dependencies, optional extras (`semantic`, `ui`, `dev`), console script entry point, ruff/mypy/pytest config.
column4: Two critical settings: (1) `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel].include = ["src/theo/skills/theo/*.md"]` -- bundles skill markdown into the wheel so `importlib.resources` finds it at runtime; (2) `[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]` exempts `src/theo/cli/ui.py` from E501 because of its embedded HTML template. The strict-mode mypy override for `theo.cli.ui` accommodates Flask's optional-extra status in CI. Dependabot pins narrow version windows (often single-version) for `real-ladybug`, `mcp`, `typer`, `pytest`, `mypy`; bumps land as individual PRs.

column3: User-facing docs: what Theo is, two-layer storage model, install via `uv tool install`, `theo use` quickstart, UI, dev workflow.
Instruction file for coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor). Tells the agent to use the theo skill when planning and updating the graph, and to register new discoveries in node/edge notes.
column4: Content under the `# Theo` heading is kept in sync by `theo use` (`_update_agents_md`), which strips and rewrites that section on each run. Source of truth is `AGENTS_MD_SECTION` in `src/theo/cli/use.py`.

column3: uv lockfile pinning all transitive dependencies.
Apache-2.0 license.
Git ignore rules. `theo use` idempotently appends `.theo/db/` to make the runtime DB gitignored.
Pre-commit hooks: ruff (lint + format), mypy (strict), markdownlint.
Markdownlint rules used by pre-commit.
GitHub Actions CI: lint, type-check, unit tests. Fast tier (no integration markers).
GitHub Actions integration workflow: integration and e2e tests that need a real KuzuDB and the installed CLI.
Dependabot config for dependency updates.
`theo reload` -- rebuild the runtime KuzuDB from the on-disk CSV files. Use after manual edits to `.theo/*.csv` (or pulling changes that touch them).
column4: Unconditionally drops the `.db` and `.db.wal` and re-imports every CSV via `rebuild_from_csv`. Requires both `concepts.csv` and `source_files.csv` to be present (empty is fine); other relationship CSVs are optional at import time. Does NOT lock against a concurrently-running `theo serve`: since every MCP tool call opens its own connection, the race window is small, but a reload during an in-flight query may still error out -- this is a user-triggered operation and accepted as such.
